name: Pine Belt
description_short: Pine Belt Federal Credit Union is a nonprofit, member-owned credit union serving Hattiesburg and New Augusta, MS since 1942, offering savings accounts, loans, ATM access, and financial advisory services.
description_long:
Pine Belt Federal Credit Union has operated as a community-focused financial institution in the Pine Belt area of Mississippi for nearly 80 years, since its founding in 1942. The credit union maintains two physical branch locations—one in Hattiesburg and one in New Augusta—and serves members who live, work, worship, or attend school in Forrest or Perry County. As a nonprofit, member-owned institution insured by the NCUA, Pine Belt returns annual profits to members through improved services and dividends rather than distributing earnings to external shareholders.

Pine Belt offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including checking and savings accounts, share certificates (CDs) with competitive rates and low minimum balances, auto loans with terms up to 84 months, personal loans, credit cards, and overdraft protection services. The credit union provides 24-hour ATM access, direct payroll deposit options, no-cost notary services, and access to financial advisors for personalized guidance. Members can apply for membership and loans online, and the institution emphasizes quick and easy application processes.

The credit union distinguishes itself through its explicit nonprofit structure and community focus, contrasting itself directly with for-profit banks on their website. Pine Belt emphasizes treating members as individuals rather than "faceless numbers" and prioritizes member voice in institutional governance. The organization highlights competitive interest rates on savings accounts, low loan fees, and auto loan rates described as "some of the lowest" available locally. Their overdraft protection is available at rates as low as $30 per item.

As a credit union, Pine Belt's primary limitation is geographic—membership is restricted to those connected to Forrest or Perry County through residence, employment, education, or worship. The website does not provide detailed information about all fee structures, specific APR ranges for different loan types, or minimum deposit requirements beyond mentioning "low minimum balances" for CDs. Prospective members must contact the institution directly for granular pricing details, and the institution's smaller scale compared to national banks may offer fewer digital features or branch access.
services: Share certificates (CDs) with low minimum balances
Auto loans with up to 84-month terms
pros: Nonprofit structure returns annual profits to members through improved services and dividends
Auto loans available with terms up to 84-month options and competitive rates
No-cost notary services for all members
Overdraft protection available as low as $30 per item
Share certificates (CDs) with low minimum balances and competitive interest rates
Nearly 80-year track record serving the Pine Belt community since 1942
cons: Membership restricted to those living, working, worshiping, or attending school in Forrest or Perry County only
Limited to two physical branch locations (Hattiesburg and New Augusta)
Website lacks detailed fee schedules, specific APR ranges, and minimum deposit amounts beyond general descriptions
No information provided about mobile app features or digital banking capabilities
Limited geographic footprint may reduce convenience for members outside immediate service area
best_for: Residents and employees of Forrest and Perry County, Mississippi seeking lower fees and competitive rates
Members wanting a nonprofit financial institution that returns profits and offers member governance participation
Local borrowers seeking competitive auto loans with extended terms and personal loan options
Community-focused savers prioritizing NCUA insurance and local reinvestment over national bank networks
diagnosis: Pine Belt Federal Credit Union is best for residents and employees of Forrest or Perry County, Mississippi who value nonprofit membership structure, local community focus, and competitive rates over national bank convenience. The primary caveat is strict geographic membership eligibility—you must live, work, worship, or attend school in the service area to join.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
